events.name: The Composio Blueprint: Inside the Mind of an AI Infra Founder
events.shortDescription: Soham Ganatra (Composio) and Manav Garg unpack how Composio built infra and tooling for 50+ AI startups.
events.longDescription:
The Composio Blueprint: Inside the Mind of an AI Infra Founder
Join us for a conversation with Soham Ganatra, Co-founder of Composio, and Manav Garg, Co-founder of Together Fund, as they unpack how Composio is powering the agentic AI revolution from India to the Valley, and what it takes to build a developer-first AI infra company with a global GTM. 

Composio is a San Francisco- and Bengaluru-based AI infrastructure startup that gives developers the “skill layer” for AI agents — helping LLMs connect, automate, and act across hundreds of enterprise tools. They are building the integration backbone for the agent-powered enterprise of the future. They also announced the launch of Rube, their MCP server, recently.
This conversation will unpack:
Building deep tech in India with a Valley-native mindset
Crafting a GTM playbook from India to the US
Designing business models for AI infra vs. AI apps
Raising capital from top SF investors while building from India
Charting Composio’s journey alongside fast-moving frontier-model companies
Who should attend?
Founders & product leaders building AI-native or infra-first startups
Developers, PMs, and GTM leaders scaling global AI products from India
Event Flow:
5PM - Doors open
5:30PM-6:45 PM - Fireside chat with Q&A
6:45PM-8PM - Networking & High Tea

Note: We'd appreciate if everyone is seated in the auditorium by 5:15 PM latest so we can begin on time!

📍 Location: Samarthanam Auditorium, HSR Layout

events.name: Runtogether Hackathon
events.shortDescription: 10-hour in-person execution sprint in Bangalore. Idea to demo to investor pitch in one day; top teams fast-tracked to SwarmSpace.
events.longDescription:
Event Description
RunTogether is a 10-hour, in-person startup execution sprint in Bangalore.
In a single day, solo founders and small teams (2–3) go from an idea to a working product demo to an investor pitch deck using runable.com
Top teams pitch live to Together Fund, with winners earning a fast-track to SwarmSpace (up to $1M funding opportunity) plus cash, Runable subscriptions, and more.
Partners
Official LLM Partner: Anthropic 
Official Voice AI Partner: Eleven Labs
Official Payments Partner: Stripe
Key Details
📍 Location: Bangalore (In-person only)
⏱ Duration: 10 hours (single day)
👥 Team size: Solo founders or teams of 2–3
🛠 What you build:
Working product demo
Launch-ready landing website
Basic brand kit
Investor pitch deck
🍽️ Food (Lunch, Dinner, & Snacks) & loads of Diet Coke and Beer available on site
Who Should Apply
This event is for people who ship, not just ideate.
You should apply if you are:
A solo founder or early-stage startup team
An engineer, designer, or operator who can build end-to-end
A student serious about startups and execution
What Makes RunTogether Different
Most hackathons reward ideas and participation; RunTogether rewards execution, real demos, investor-level clarity, and products that can actually survive beyond the event.
By the end of the day, teams walk away with something they can show users, show investors, and keep building after the sprint.
Rules & Constraints
All work must be done within the 10-hour event
Pre-built assets are not allowed
Pre-existing ideas are allowed
Teams must use Runable to build core assets
External APIs/tools may be used (TBD)
Judges must be able to access demos and links
Application & Approval
This event is application-based.
Limited seats
Teams are shortlisted based on execution ability and clarity
Approved participants will receive confirmation via email/WhatsApp
Please apply only if you can attend in-person for the full duration.

About Together Fund
Together Fund is India’s only operator-led venture capital firm with presence across India and US. With over $200M in AUM, Together backs ambitious founders building global-first companies.
About Runable
runable.com is a design-driven, general AI agent that helps you go from idea to finished output in minutes.
Build websites, presentations, images, videos, documents, and more in one place, no tool-hopping, just outcomes.

events.name: Mumbai Fintech Happy Hour | 28th Aug, 2024
events.shortDescription: Fintech community mixer in Mumbai co-hosted with This Week in Fintech, AWS, Lex Consult, Cockroach Labs, and MarsShot VC.
events.featured: Co-hosted with This Week in Fintech
events.longDescription:
Hi fintech friends!

Please join us for the Mumbai Fintech Happy Hour brought to you by AWS, Lex Consult, Cockroach Labs and MarsShotVC, hosted by This Week in Fintech! We are excited to host you on Wednesday, 28th August at BKC, Mumbai along the sidelines of Global Fintech Fest.
If you're attending the Global Fintech Fest the Fintech Happy Hour is going to be the best sundowner meetup to attend. 

Event Details:
- Date: Wednesday, 28th Aug, 2024
- Time: 6pm to 9pm
- Venue: BKC, Mumbai (venue details will be shared with approved guests).
Agenda: 
- 6:00pm: registration
- 6:30pm: panel discussion
- 7:00pm to 09:00pm: snacks and cocktails
Big thanks to our sponsors: 
- AWS: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud, offering over 200 fully featured services from data centers globally.
- Lex Consult: Lex Consult is a next-generation law firm representing founders, corporates, investors and institutions, on corporate transactions, regulatory and advisory matters across sectors. The Firm’s fintech practice has been ranked as one of the best in the country by Chambers and Partners in 2024. The Firm has been involved in significant transactions and collaborations in the fintech sector in the last few years.
- Cockroach Labs: Cockroach Labs is the creator of CockroachDB, the cloud-native, distributed SQL database designed to enable global businesses to build and operate world-changing applications. CockroachDB is trusted to run mission-critical workloads for some of the world's largest enterprises, including JP Morgan, Netflix, Bose, Comcast, Hard Rock Digital, Fortune 50 financial institutions, as well as category leaders in retail and media.
- MarsShot.VC: MarsShot.VC is an investment initiative by Razorpay’s Founding members who believe that for India to be a $10Tn economy we need the startup ecosystem to increase its impact manifolds. MarsShot actively invests in and supports early-stage startups based on what the team has learnt from the Razorpay journey. Their portfolio of 85+ companies includes some of the best startups doing extraordinary work across Fintech, SaaS, Consumer Internet, D2C, Healthtech, Sustainability and AI fields.
Who is this event for?
- Founders in fintech, DeFi, insurtech and others
- Product managers, marketers, designers etc.
- Investors
- Senior executives at regulated financial firms
- Industry participants (legal firms, CA firms, and so on)
- Folks wanting to switch from traditional financial firms to fintech startups
Space is limited for the Happy Hour.
Would you like to be a sponsor? Email me

We look forward to seeing you soon! In the meantime, check out our weekly summary of the front page of Asia fintech news here.

Our events in other cities are coming soon!

Fintech Happy Hour is hosted by This Week in Fintech and Osborne Saldanha.

events.name: From Human Video to Robotic Action - How to Build General-Purpose Robots
events.shortDescription: Co-hosted with Paras Chopra and Pratyush Choudhary (Lossfunk).

events.name: Building with Agents: Inside Modern AI Orchestration⚡️Powered by AWS
events.shortDescription: Online deep-dive into multi-agent orchestration patterns — retrievers, planners, executors. Powered by AWS.
events.longDescription:
Building with Agents: Inside the Emerging Patterns of Multi-Agent Orchestration
As AI systems evolve, single-model pipelines are giving way to multi-agent orchestration — where retrievers, planners, and executors work together to perform complex tasks autonomously.
This session, in collaboration with AWS, explores the emerging design patterns behind building such agentic workflows that are scalable, cost-efficient, and production-ready.
🧩 What We’ll Cover
Comparing different approaches to multi-agent orchestration — from RAG-driven agents to optimized fine-tuning.
Designing agentic systems that balance context transfer, compatibility, latency, and cost.
Key architectural considerations for building robust agentic workflows on modern cloud infrastructure.
Real-world examples of how teams are using AWS tools and frameworks to enable adaptive, modular AI systems.
💬 Interactive Q&A
The final 30 minutes will be open for discussion — a chance to ask Sudhanshu anything on building, deploying, or scaling your own agentic workflows.
Bring your questions, share what you’re building, and join a community shaping the next frontier of applied AI.
👤 Meet Sudhanshu Hate (AI Principal Architect, AWS)
With over 27 years in AI architecture and platform engineering, Sudhanshu leads AWS initiatives in Generative and Agentic AI — helping 100+ startups and enterprises design intelligent, multi-agent systems. His expertise spans RAG, prompt engineering, fine-tuning, and MLOps, making him one of the most practical voices in bringing agentic workflows to production.
🌎 About Together: 
Together Fund is India’s only operator-led venture capital firm with presence across India and US. With over $200M in AUM, Together backs ambitious founders building global-first companies, with a strong focus on AI and deep tech.
Together’s portfolio comprises 35+ companies, including Emergent, Composio, Rapid Claims, Presentations AI, Spry Health, and Rocket to name a few.

Note: By registering, you consent to Together fund sharing your contact details with AWS for updates and offers via email, post, or phone. You can unsubscribe anytime following the instructions in their communications. Your information will be handled per their Privacy Notices.

Here are some details:

⏰ Time: 5 to 6 PM onwards

👤 About the speaker
Ashwin Rajeeva co-founded Acceldata to tackle today’s toughest data challenges with AI. As CTO, he leads the development of scalable observability and governance platforms that help enterprises automate root-cause analysis, optimize resource utilization, and accelerate time-to-insight—all without ballooning engineering teams.

events.name: Inside Your Digital DNA: A Talk on User Embeddings & Truly Personal AI
events.shortDescription: Drew Kirchhoff (ShareD) on how user embeddings power truly personal AI assistants.
events.longDescription:
How can a tiny cloud of numbers capture your tastes, friendships, even your future health risks—then feed that insight to an AI assistant that plans your future?

Join Drew Kirchhoff — Founder, ShareDot & ex‑Algo PM & Data Scientist, TikTok to unpack the craft of user embeddings: the slim, multidimensional vectors that drive every “For You” feed, music recommendation and personalised copilot.

Here are some details:
📆 Date: 1st Aug, 2025
  ⏰ Time: 5 to 6 PM onwards
  📍 Location: Together Fund, Indiranagar, Bangalore

👤 About the speaker
Drew shipped recommender‑system features at TikTok that now reach more than a billion people. At ShareDot he’s building infrastructure that lets any startup drop user vectors into its stack—no MLOps team required.

events.name: From Imitation to Intelligence: Lessons from Autonomous Driving to Humanoids
events.shortDescription: Srikanth Vidapanakal (xSpecies AI) on porting autonomous-driving lessons into general-purpose robotics.
events.longDescription:
What does it take to move from teaching cars to drive themselves to enabling robots that can walk, manipulate objects and reason about the world?

Join Srikanth Vidapanakal — Founder & CEO, xSpecies AI & ex‑Director of AI, Ola Electric for a deep‑dive into the algorithms, architectures and hard‑won lessons that connect today’s autonomous‑driving breakthroughs with tomorrow’s whole‑body intelligent humanoids.

🔍 What we’ll cover
Evolution of Learning: why imitation, self‑supervision and reinforcement each matter at different stages of capability building.
Part I – Autonomous DrivingSee how self‑driving moved from separate modules to one big “camera‑to‑steering” neural network, using bird’s‑eye‑view transformers and attention tricks for perception and control.
We’ll also cover easy‑to‑grasp reinforcement‑learning tweaks that make cars drive safely in messy real‑world traffic.
Part II – Humanoids & Whole‑Body IntelligenceWe’ll apply those learning ideas to robots with arms and legs, treating walking, grabbing and navigating as one combined problem.
You’ll hear how imitation learning plus smart reward rules (like “stay balanced” and “repeat steps”) train robots to move smoothly and work with humans.
👤 About the speaker
Srikanth Vidapanakal is a seasoned technologist with 24+ years of experience & he most recently served as Director of AI at Ola Electric before founding xSpecies AI, a Bangalore‑based startup building a full‑stack platform for general‑purpose humanoids.

events.name: Dropout. Three Pivots. Scaling Fast: The Metaforms Story
events.shortDescription: Akshat Tyagi (Metaforms CEO) and Manav Garg on going from dropout to fast-scaling AI company in 18 months.
events.featured: Akshat Tyagi (Co-founder & CEO, Metaforms) with Manav Garg
events.longDescription:
Dropout. Three Pivots. Scaling Fast: The Metaforms Story
Join us for a conversation with Akshat Tyagi, Co-founder & CEO of Metaforms, and Manav Garg, Co-founder of Together Fund, as they talk about how Metaforms is reinventing market research with AI and what Akshat's early playbook looks like - as a founder who didn't have a manual.
Metaforms gives research agencies AI superpowers: automating survey programming, data processing, vendor coordination, voice research, and reporting. In just six months, it has onboarded four of the world’s top 20 agencies, processes 1,000+ surveys a month, and is reshaping a $130B global industry.
This conversation will unpack:
Taking unconventional bets—dropping out, pivoting, and navigating uncertainty
Moving early in AI and breaking into market research with an outsider lens
Winning trust—across investors, teammates, and customers
Building a category-defining giant in a niche, 'uncool' market
Who should attend?
Founders & product leaders building AI-native platforms
GTM & ops leaders scaling AI products from India to global markets
Marketing professionals exploring AI’s role in speed, scale & efficiency
Event Flow:
5PM - Doors open
5:30PM-6:45 PM - Fireside chat with Q&A
6:45OM-8PM - Networking & High Tea

events.name: Imagination and Composition: Building the next generation of AI reasoning systems
events.shortDescription: Amit Sharma (Microsoft Research) on pushing LLMs from pattern recognition into composable reasoning.
events.longDescription:
How do we push large language models beyond pattern recognition into true reasoning, enabling them to imagine “what if” scenarios and compose ideas into long, complex chains?
Join us for a deep-dive into the next frontier of AI: imagination and composition. This talk will explore the current limits of today’s systems, what’s missing, and how novel techniques like axiomatic training and reasoning monitors can unlock reliable, long-running reasoning in LLMs. Expect insights at the intersection of science, engineering, and product impact.
Here are some details:
📆 Date: 28 Aug, 2025
⏰ Time: 5:30 PM Onwards
📍 Location: Together Fund, Indiranagar, Bangalore
👤 About the speaker
Amit Sharma (Principal Researcher, Microsoft) brings cutting-edge research and practical expertise in building reasoning-capable AI systems. Their work focuses on advancing beyond narrow pattern-matching into models that can imagine, compose, and sustain multi-step reasoning paving the way for breakthroughs in both science and business.

events.shortDescription: Talk on what actually scales - model size, test-time thinking, or data.
